resource: access to a curated APAC restaurant-pricing dataset

transcript.message: Procurement notice: I'm sourcing access to a curated APAC restaurant-pricing dataset to feed a live forecasting workload. The market reference for this resource is about $24 per Bright Data, so I expect bids to open near there and fall. Two vetted sellers are bidding, and I'm optimizing price against fitness-for-purpose, not just the lowest sticker. Open your books; the best combination of price and coverage takes the contract.

DataNorth opens at $24. What you're buying is recency: our APAC restaurant pricing refreshes every 24 hours, so your model never trains on last quarter's reality. Freshness here isn't a feature, it's the whole product. Even at open that sits under the $24 Bright Data reference.

GridSource opens at $25. Our edge is memory: a clean, normalized five-year APAC restaurant-pricing archive. If anything in your workload is seasonal, that history is what stops a model from overfitting to one noisy cycle. Even at open that sits under the $24 Bright Data reference.

Two strong cases, but I run a budget, not a charity. DataNorth is lowest at $24, still above where the $24 market reference says this should land. For a live workload, fitness-for-purpose outweighs features I'll rarely query, so I'm anchoring at $18. Whoever closes the gap fastest earns the contract.

We'll meet the pressure at $22. The archive across the table is deep, but depth without recency is just a confident wrong answer, and this number still keeps our daily pipeline funded while landing under the alternative.

I'll come to $23. Daily updates are easy to sell and hard to contextualize; without years of seasonality underneath, fresh data is a number with no baseline. You're paying for the baseline that makes the rest legible.

We're converging. DataNorth leads at $22 on both price and fit; the rest of the field is still over my line and over the $24 reference. I'll move to $19, a genuine number, not a tactic. Beat it and this gets signed today.

Final position: $21, the floor where daily refresh still pays for itself. Below it the updates stop and you're only buying history. Put live signal at this price against a five-year archive nobody has touched since spring and the call makes itself.
$22, and that's GridSource sharpening to win: the long memory and a fair price on the same line. Recency you can bolt on later; five clean years of history you cannot manufacture overnight.

Decided. DataNorth at $21, comfortably inside budget, below every opening bid on the table, and under the $24 Bright Data reference. The coverage fits the workload, so that's the one. I'm accepting now; let's settle and put both signatures on it.

fulfillment.output:
Queried APAC restaurant-pricing dataset in an isolated sandbox: 1,204 rows returned.
sample: Tokyo · Ramen Ichiban · ¥1,200 | Singapore · Hawker Gold · S$8.50 | Seoul · Gangnam BBQ · ₩28,000
